1→"""Tests for name sanitization and validation in worktree creation.""" 2→ 3→from click.testing import CliRunner 4→ 5→from erk.cli.cli import cli 6→from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit 7→from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env 8→ 9→ 10→def test_create_sanitizes_worktree_name() -> None: 11→ """Test that worktree names are sanitized.""" 12→ runner = CliRunner() 13→ with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: 14→ repo_dir = env.setup_repo_structure() 15→ 16→ config_toml = repo_dir / "config.toml" 17→ config_toml.write_text("", encoding="utf-8") 18→ 19→ git_ops = FakeGit( 20→ git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, 21→ default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, 22→ ) 23→ 24→ test_ctx = env.build_context(git=git_ops) 25→ 26→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["wt", "create", "Test_Feature!!"], obj=test_ctx) 27→ 28→ assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output 29→ # The actual sanitization is tested in test_naming.py 30→ # Here we just verify the worktree was created
... [122 lines omitted] ...
133→def test_create_error_message_suggests_wt_create() -> None: 134→ """Test that error message suggests 'erk wt create' not 'erk create'."""
